page: OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Storage is bundled with a 100 GB local NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is provisioned at a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance runs on the KVM hypervisor and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is stable, registering 117.7 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 75.43 GB/sec for large block write operations. However, static web serving throughput is relatively weak at 4.55 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 25% of tested configurations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the b3-16 offers dedicated AMD EPYC resources and bundled NVMe storage without the premium of specialized memory or compute-heavy instances. This makes it a balanced option for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without hyperthreading interference. Suitable workloads include text processing, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: The OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose server featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor clocked at 2.3 GHz. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-based instance provides 16.0 GB of RAM and includes a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain average. However, static web serving throughput is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. Lacking hyperthreading, the dedicated core allocation ensures consistent execution. This resource profile makes the b3-16 highly suitable for software compilation, text processing, database operations, and general-purpose application hosting where predictable CPU performance is required.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose server with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for databases and general workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 16 GB RAM, and 100 GB of bundled NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and bundled NVMe storage for predictable, non-hyperthreaded computing workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading
16.0 GB of system memory and 100 GB of bundled NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and one public IPv4 address
Well-suited for software compilation, databases, and general-purpose hosting
